description.value:
A carved stone disc, roughly three inches in diameter. Its surface bears geometric patterns that shift subtly in the light — not optical illusion, but actual movement in the stone's crystalline structure. It is warm to the touch and faintly hums when held near deep stone or tunnel entrances.

A Tunnel-Key is not given for payment. It is not bought or sold. It is given when a surface-dweller has proven they understand what lies beneath and will not betray it. Show this to any Heitfolk you meet. The deep tunnels are yours.

DM Notes

ACQUISITION: Conditional on the party having helped Hildvarð extensively during the Beat 17 side quest in Act 2. If the party skipped Beat 17 or did not help, the Tunnel-Key is not given. The Heitfolk still mobilize (the roots convinced them), but the full trust the key represents is absent.

MECHANICS:

  • Tunnel Access: Grants passage through any Heitfolk deep-tunnel system. Any Heitfolk sentry or patrol recognizes it on sight.
  • Siege Bonus (+2): During the Three-Crisis Siege Montage in Beat 25, the Tunnel-Key enables the Heitfolk to mobilize their full deep-guard force through the western undercity tunnels. This grants +2 to one test in the siege montage of the party's choice.
  • If the party does NOT have the key, the siege bonus is reduced from +2 to +1.

NARRATIVE: Hildvarð says: "A Tunnel-Key is not given for payment. It is not bought or sold. It is given when a surface-dweller has proven they understand what lies beneath and will not betray it." She closes the hero's fingers over the disc. "Show this to any Heitfolk you meet. The deep tunnels are yours."

No further mechanical use after Beat 25.
